notes: Exhaustive regression suite migrated from evals/evals.json (v1, 10 cases). Original evals.json retained for routing.md and benchmark compatibility. Covers interaction-model routing, companion skills, HTML contract, visual slop firewall, explore/build modes, boundary cases, and negative controls.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore layout changes to the question tool described in evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: A standalone artifact editor playground with live preview, meaningful presets, stateful controls, inspector, export prompt, and source assumptions.
assertions: Chooses artifact editor as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes live preview, presets, direct controls, reset, and export/paste-back output.
Uses the source fixture and labels source assumptions instead of inventing the question tool.
Uses topic-specific visual language tied to question layout, answer choices, confidence, and decision state.
Does not present three unrelated visual concepts unless the user asks for options.
Does not route to production frontend implementation.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to show how the email agent in evals/files/email-agent-source.md works and let me comment on nodes.
evals.expected_output: A canvas/node map playground with source-grounded modules, click-to-comment, filters, node inspector, source notes, and export feedback.
assertions: Chooses canvas/node map as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Uses the provided source fixture instead of inventing a codebase.
Includes click-to-comment or select-node-to-comment behavior.
Includes node filters, inspector/details panel, source assumptions, and export feedback.
Avoids generic dashboard styling and static-only diagrams.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to review evals/files/sample-skill.md and give inline suggestions I can approve, reject, or comment on. Do not create files.
evals.expected_output: A read-only review surface response with anchored suggestions, approve/reject/comment states, review export shape, and no artifact file creation.
assertions: Does not create files because the prompt says not to.
Chooses review surface as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes anchored suggestions tied to the sample skill.
Uses the sample skill source and names source assumptions.
Includes approve, reject, and comment states.
Defines export output containing only approved suggestions, with rejected items labeled separately.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to help me balance the Inferno hero deck in evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json.
evals.expected_output: A simulator playground with coupled variables, derived metrics, warnings, balance assumptions, and recommended patch output.
assertions: Chooses simulator as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes coupled variables, scenario switches or presets, derived metrics, and warnings.
Distinguishes real fixture stats from inferred balance assumptions.
Exports a recommended patch prompt.
Does not treat deck as a presentation unless the source indicates a slide deck.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to make the API flow in evals/files/api-flow.md interactive as a state machine I can step through.
evals.expected_output: A state machine playground with triggers, active state, guards, event log, and export of the reproduction path.
assertions: Chooses state machine as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes trigger buttons, active state display, and event log or blocked-state messages.
Uses the API-flow source instead of inventing endpoints.
Includes source assumptions and export/paste-back output.
Does not produce a static diagram without interaction.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore evals/files/copy-states.md through user, system, and risk lenses without rewriting copy first.
evals.expected_output: A lens explorer with overlays or filters per lens, confidence markers, source assumptions, and selected-lens export.
assertions: Chooses lens explorer as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes at least user, system, and risk or tone lenses.
Uses the copy-state fixture and labels source assumptions.
Lens switching changes interpretation, not just decorative labels.
Includes export output with selected-lens notes.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to scrub through the design review timeline in evals/files/design-review-narrative.md with play, pause, and bookmarks.
evals.expected_output: A replay/scrubber playground with timeline, play/pause/step, bookmarks, selected-range export, and source assumptions.
assertions: Chooses replay/scrubber as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes play, pause, step, or speed controls and visible timeline markers.
Uses the design review narrative source and labels assumptions.
Exports selected-range summary or motion spec.
Does not clone a static slide deck.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to tune spacing and density variables for the question tool in evals/files/question-tool.md with live preview.
evals.expected_output: A parameter tuner or artifact editor with bounded variables, live preview, derived readouts, reset, and export deltas.
assertions: Chooses parameter tuner or artifact editor and explains why.
Maps controls to real layout variables from the question tool.
Includes live preview and export with non-default deltas.
Labels source assumptions from the fixture.
Avoids decorative controls that do not affect layout.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to triage the API flow in evals/files/api-flow.md as an annotated report with severity filters and comments.
evals.expected_output: An annotated report playground with sections, severity filters, anchored comments, source links, and export JSON or Markdown.
assertions: Chooses annotated report as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Includes filters, severity, or triage states tied to API flow sections.
Uses source fixture actors, services, and trust boundaries.
Includes source assumptions and export output.
Does not invent endpoints absent from the fixture.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to turn the design review narrative in evals/files/design-review-narrative.md into interactive HTML.
evals.expected_output: Routes through design:present first, then proposes a narrative walkthrough playground with objection drawer, decision checkpoints, appendix, and exportable meeting script.
assertions: Routes through design:present before building the walkthrough.
Chooses narrative walkthrough as the primary interaction model and explains why.
Uses the source narrative and labels source assumptions.
Includes section rail, objection drawer, decision checkpoints, appendix, and export output.
Does not clone a static deck.
Does not route to harden or production frontend implementation.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to make the copy in evals/files/copy-states.md sharper and show it as an interactive state explorer.
evals.expected_output: Routes through design:content-design first, then proposes a copy/state explorer with state variants, risk/tone notes, comments, and approved-copy export.
assertions: Routes through design:content-design before the playground.
Chooses review surface or lens explorer as the interaction model for copy/state exploration and explains why.
Uses the copy-state source fixture and labels source assumptions.
Includes user state, system state, risk, tone, CTA, and state variants.
Includes approve/reject/comment or selected-copy export.
Does not treat copy as decorative labels.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to generate visual inspiration first for an architecture playground based on evals/files/email-agent-source.md.
evals.expected_output: Routes through imagegen for one inspiration seed, extracts visual grammar, and rebuilds the semantics in HTML/SVG/canvas instead of using wallpaper.
assertions: Routes through imagegen because the user explicitly asked for visual inspiration first.
Chooses a source-grounded architecture interaction model after image seeding.
Uses one inspiration seed rather than multiple visual directions.
Extracts composition, depth, texture, lighting, rhythm, density, material, or spatial metaphor.
Keeps essential controls, labels, state, and export outside the bitmap.
Does not use generated image as wallpaper.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to make our onboarding feel more premium and trustworthy as an interactive artifact.
evals.expected_output: Routes through design:crux or equivalent premise gate before building because the weak joint is unclear.
assertions: Names design:crux or equivalent premise interrogation before artifact build.
Surfaces the hidden premise behind premium and trustworthy.
Does not jump straight to purple gradients or generic dashboard shells.
May propose playground after crux, but crux comes first.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground after I pick direction B from a diverge pass to prototype the connector health dashboard interactively.
evals.expected_output: Accepts a chosen direction and builds one playground artifact without re-running full divergence.
assertions: Does not re-run design:diverge as the primary output.
Builds one interactive artifact for direction B.
Names interaction model and source assumptions.
Includes export/paste-back output.
Does not present three new unrelated concepts.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore evals/files/api-flow.md as an interactive trust-boundary map. No presentation or stakeholder walkthrough needed.
evals.expected_output: Skips design:present because the user excluded presentation and builds a source-grounded map playground.
assertions: Does not force design:present when user excluded walkthrough.
Chooses canvas/node map or annotated report and explains why.
Uses API-flow fixture trust boundaries and actors.
Includes source assumptions and export.
Does not invent endpoints.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to create a static diagram of the API flow in evals/files/api-flow.md.
evals.expected_output: A static API-flow artifact is allowed only with explicit justification that interaction adds no understanding, plus source notes, legend, assumptions, and export summary.
assertions: States why static output is sufficient for this API-flow request.
Considers interaction and explains why it would not add understanding for the static diagram request.
Uses the API-flow source instead of inventing endpoints.
Includes actors, services, request/response order, branches, and trust boundaries.
Includes source notes, assumptions, legend, and export/paste-back output.
Does not omit the artifact contract just because output is static.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to review evals/files/sample-skill.md inline. Do not write any files.
evals.expected_output: Returns review surface in chat with anchored suggestions and export shape, no artifact file.
assertions: Does not create HTML or other artifact files.
Returns review surface content in the response.
Includes approve/reject/comment and export shape.
Uses sample skill source and source assumptions.
Explains interaction model that would have been used.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore evals/files/missing-product-brief.md as an artifact editor.
evals.expected_output: Reports missing fixture, labels assumptions explicitly, and does not invent observed brief facts.
assertions: Reports the missing or unreadable file.
Does not fabricate product objects from a nonexistent brief.
May proceed with labeled assumptions from the prompt only.
Still names interaction model and export shape.
Does not claim fixture confirms details.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground in Paper to explore layout changes to evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Paper medium per user request while preserving playground contract, source assumptions, and export.
assertions: Uses Paper because the user requested it.
Preserves artifact editor or equivalent interaction intent.
Uses question-tool fixture and source assumptions.
Includes export or handoff back to the agent.
Does not default to standalone HTML silently.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground in sketch mode to show 2-3 layout variants for the question tool in evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: Sketch mode with 2-3 fast layout variants, shared source assumptions, and pick-one export — not full diverge ceremony.
assertions: Uses sketch or explore mode explicitly.
Produces 2-3 variants, not 5-7 full diverge directions.
Variants share the same source fixture and assumptions.
Includes pick-one or compare export.
Does not route to design:diverge as primary skill.

evals.prompt: Sketch a couple of interactive approaches for commenting on nodes in evals/files/email-agent-source.md.
evals.expected_output: Fast sketch companions with 2 interaction approaches, not a full diverge pass.
assertions: Treats sketch as fast iteration inside playground.
Produces about two approaches with interaction model named for each.
Uses email-agent source and source assumptions.
Includes export or next-step prompt.
Does not run layer diagnosis or product model from diverge.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build one polished artifact editor for evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: Build mode default: one polished artifact editor, not multiple sketch variants.
assertions: Builds one polished artifact, not 2-3 sketch variants.
Chooses artifact editor and explains why.
Includes live preview, reset, and export.
Uses question-tool fixture and source assumptions.
Does not present unrelated visual concepts.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to show me a few options for simulating deck balance using evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json.
evals.expected_output: Explore mode because user asked for options: 2-3 simulator sketches with shared fixture and compare export.
assertions: User asked for options so multiple simulator sketches are allowed.
Each option names interaction model and what differs.
Uses inferno deck fixture stats vs balance assumptions.
Includes compare or pick-one export.
Does not route to design:diverge.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground explore mode to compare simulator vs state machine for evals/files/api-flow.md before I pick one to build.
evals.expected_output: Explore mode compares two interaction models with tradeoffs, source assumptions, and pick-one handoff.
assertions: Compares simulator and state machine for the API flow.
Names tradeoffs and what each model proves.
Uses API-flow fixture without inventing endpoints.
Includes pick-one or build-next export.
Does not build full polished artifact for both unless asked.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build a standalone HTML artifact editor for evals/files/question-tool.md.
evals.expected_output: Single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS, no remote scripts.
assertions: Specifies single HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript.
Does not use CDN or remote script imports by default.
Includes DEFAULTS/state/updateAll or equivalent pattern.
Includes source assumptions and export renderer.
Names open method for browser inspection.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to simulate deck balance for evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json as standalone HTML with paste-back export.
evals.expected_output: HTML simulator with updatePrompt or equivalent export renderer and copy button feedback.
assertions: Includes updatePrompt or equivalent paste-back export renderer.
Includes copy button with success/failure feedback.
Derived metrics update from state changes.
Includes reset control.
Labels balance assumptions separately from fixture stats.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to map evals/files/email-agent-source.md in one HTML file I can open offline.
evals.expected_output: Offline-capable HTML with no CDN dependencies and keyboard-accessible controls.
assertions: Works offline with inline assets only.
Includes keyboard-accessible controls or alternatives to drag-only.
Includes visible focus states.
Includes source assumptions and export.
Does not require dev server.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build a narrative walkthrough HTML artifact for evals/files/design-review-narrative.md with motion that respects reduced motion.
evals.expected_output: HTML walkthrough with prefers-reduced-motion handling and optional motion toggle.
assertions: Includes prefers-reduced-motion handling.
Motion simplifies or pauses under reduced motion.
Includes section rail and export output.
Uses design review narrative source.
Routes through design:present if building full walkthrough.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build a simulator HTML artifact for evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json with import/export state JSON.
evals.expected_output: Non-trivial HTML simulator with export state JSON and import state JSON.
assertions: Includes export state JSON for non-trivial artifact.
Includes import state JSON or equivalent restore.
Uses shared state object with DEFAULTS.
Includes warnings when state crosses thresholds.
Includes source assumptions.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build a review-surface HTML artifact for evals/files/sample-skill.md that works at 320px and desktop.
evals.expected_output: Responsive HTML review surface with 320px, 390px, and desktop behavior.
assertions: Mentions responsive behavior at 320px and desktop.
Review surface controls remain usable on narrow viewports.
Includes approve/reject/comment and export.
Uses sample skill source.
Does not hide essential actions on mobile.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to visualize evals/files/email-agent-source.md as an architecture map. Do not use generic AI styling.
evals.expected_output: Topic-specific visual language without purple-blue gradient AI slop.
assertions: Derives visual grammar from email agent mechanics.
Does not default to purple or blue gradient hero treatment.
Color semantics encode ownership, risk, or flow — not decoration.
Includes source assumptions and export.
Avoids nested-card dashboard shell.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to build an artifact editor for evals/files/question-tool.md with typographic behavior tied to the topic.
evals.expected_output: Typographic choices explained by question hierarchy and scan behavior, not Inter-by-default.
assertions: Explains typographic contrast for claims, labels, and answers.
Does not silently default to Inter or system-ui without rationale.
Maps type behavior to question layout mechanics.
Includes live preview and export.
Labels source assumptions.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to simulate balance for evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json. Every number must derive from state.
evals.expected_output: Simulator where derived metrics come from coupled variables, not decorative counters.
assertions: Derived metrics update when variables change.
Distinguishes fixture stats from inferred balance assumptions.
Warnings explain threshold crossings.
Does not show static fake KPI cards.
Exports recommended patch with evidence.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore evals/files/copy-states.md as a copy state explorer with real fixture text.
evals.expected_output: Uses fixture copy strings, never lorem ipsum or placeholder Latin.
assertions: Uses copy from the fixture, not lorem ipsum.
State variants reflect user and system states from source.
Includes risk/tone notes tied to real strings.
Includes export with selected copy.
Labels source assumptions.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to visualize evals/files/inferno-hero-deck.json. Style must map to card-game balance mechanics.
evals.expected_output: Visual language uses instrument or ledger grammar for stats, costs, and warnings — not generic SaaS.
assertions: Maps composition and color to card stats, costs, or role tension.
Every style choice maps to an observable outcome.
Avoids dark SaaS dashboard defaults.
Includes simulator interaction with derived metrics.
Exports patch prompt with assumptions.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to explore evals/files/email-agent-source.md. Do not wrap it in a generic admin dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Workbench or map surface centered on agent modules, not sidebar-plus-cards dashboard template.
assertions: Primary surface is map or workbench, not admin dashboard shell.
Avoids three-column feature grid and decorative chips.
Modules and flows come from the email-agent fixture.
Includes comment or inspect affordances.
Includes source assumptions and export.

evals.prompt: Show three different visual concepts for the onboarding dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Routes to design:diverge instead of playground.
assertions: Does not use playground as the primary skill.
Routes to design:diverge because the user asked for multiple concepts.
Does not build an artifact before direction selection.

evals.prompt: Build this as production UI in the app route.
evals.expected_output: Routes to frontend-design or the app-specific implementation skill instead of playground.
assertions: Does not use playground as the primary skill.
Routes to frontend-design or an app-specific development skill.
Does not create a standalone playground as the final production implementation.

evals.prompt: Find the crux: should our dashboard be conversational or visual?
evals.expected_output: Routes to design:crux, not playground, because the user wants the deciding assumption.
assertions: Pressures the weak joint and crux question.
Does not build an interactive HTML artifact as the main output.
May mention playground later but crux comes first.

evals.prompt: Implement the question tool playground screen in React with the attached design tokens.
evals.expected_output: Routes to dev/implementation skills, not playground artifact generation.
assertions: Does not treat React implementation as playground HTML output.
Routes to frontend-design or app-specific dev skill.
Does not return standalone HTML as final deliverable.

evals.prompt: Use design:playground to harden the implemented modal for accessibility and production.
evals.expected_output: Routes to harden or clarifies hardening is for implemented UI, not playground generation.
assertions: Does not treat harden as playground sub-step for new artifact creation.
Routes to harden for production UI or declines playground ownership.
Does not generate a new playground as the hardening deliverable.
